creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to Jeremy Chadwick from comment #0)
> I recently upgraded from bind910-9.10.6_3 to bind911-9.11.2_3 on FreeBSD
> 10.4-STABLE (stable/10, kernel/world built from r325340) and have begun to
> see this show up in my kernel logs/syslog (i.e. console, /var/log/all.log,
> dmesg, etc.) every time named is started:
> 
> Nov 10 06:51:44 mambo named[71491]: socket.c:5695: unexpected error:
> Nov 10 06:51:44 mambo named[71491]: setsockopt(21, TCP_FASTOPEN) failed with
> Protocol not available
> Nov 10 06:51:44 mambo named[71491]: socket.c:5695: unexpected error:
> Nov 10 06:51:44 mambo named[71491]: setsockopt(22, TCP_FASTOPEN) failed with
> Protocol not available
> 
> dns/bind910 does not have this problem.  I have not checked dns/bind912.
> 
> RFC7413 (TCP fast open) support is disabled (read: not included in the
> kernel) by default on FreeBSD 10.x (and also 11.x, unless that's changed
> recently) because it's considered experimental.  This system uses a kernel
> config very similar to GENERIC, and thus does not include "option
> TCP_RFC7413".
> 
> Bug 206338 is for an unrelated piece of software, but further justifies
> advocacy of disabling this feature (in 3rd-party software) by default.
> 
> Reviewing the BIND 9.11.2 source, it appears configure.in includes
> TCP_FASTOPEN auto-detection by using #include and #ifdef TCP_FASTOPEN,
> resulting in #define ISC_PLATFORM_HAVETFO 1.  FreeBSD should override this
> somehow (either through a patch that comments out the code, or better yet,
> #undef ISC_PLATFORM_HAVETFO).  I do not see an explicit configure flag for
> disabling it.  See lib/isc/unix/socket.c lines ~5684 to ~5700 and/or
> configure.in lines ~3206 to ~3222 for details.

This is a sysctl(8) tunable
net.inet.tcp.fastopen.enabled (0/1) off by default

add it to sysctl.conf(5), and bounce the box.

Apache, depending upon how it's configured, can also
throw this warning.

It might be worth mentioning this in a banner, or something
when the (bind/Apache) install completes.

HTH

--Chris
